source_access_notes.xda-developers.com: Homepage and article pages blocked (HTTP 403). Article titles, dates, and summaries retrieved via web search index. One article confirmed within 24-48h window; additional articles from May 27-28 found via search.
source_access_notes.docs.anthropic.com: Release notes pages blocked (HTTP 403). Content retrieved via web search index and official GitHub releases (github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases). Three Claude Code releases confirmed May 29-30; Opus 4.8 platform release notes confirmed May 28.
source_access_notes.theverge.com: Completely inaccessible — direct fetch blocked and domain excluded from Anthropic's web search index. Zero results available. The Verge almost certainly covered both major stories (Opus 4.8 + Series H) but no data could be retrieved.
source_access_notes.supplemental_note: Anthropic's own news page (anthropic.com/news) surfaced two additional primary-subject stories confirmed May 28, 2026: the Opus 4.8 announcement and the Series H funding round. Both are included here as update_002 and update_004 respectively, clearly sourced.

updates.title: Claude Opus 4.7 was just released last month, and 4.8 is already here with some massive improvements
updates.summary: XDA covers the release of Claude Opus 4.8 — arriving just 41 days after Opus 4.7 — highlighting benchmark gains including agentic coding improving from 64.3% to 69.2% (new state-of-the-art), multidisciplinary reasoning jumping from 54.7% to 57.9%, and knowledge work score rising from 1,753 to 1,890. The article also details the 3x cheaper Fast mode (now $10/M input tokens), the new dynamic workflows feature that orchestrates hundreds of parallel agents, and a notable improvement in honesty: Opus 4.8 is four times less likely to let code flaws pass unremarked and flags uncertainties more consistently. The piece frames the rapid release cadence as evidence of Anthropic's accelerating development pace.
updates.accuracy_notes: Benchmark numbers (64.3%→69.2%, 54.7%→57.9%, 1753→1890) corroborated by VentureBeat and Anthropic's official announcement. Fast mode pricing ($10/M input, $50/M output at 2.5x speed) confirmed across multiple outlets. The '41 days' release interval verified by TechCrunch (Opus 4.7 was released April 17, 2026). Claim about 4x reduction in uncaught code flaws should be checked against Anthropic's safety evals documentation.

updates.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
updates.summary: Anthropic's official announcement introduces Claude Opus 4.8 as having 'sharper judgment, more honesty about its progress, and the ability to work independently for longer.' Key platform additions alongside the model include: Dynamic Workflows (research preview) that orchestrates tens to hundreds of background agents for large-scale tasks via Claude Code CLI, Desktop, and VS Code; user-controlled effort levels on claude.ai; Fast mode now 3x cheaper at $10/M input and $50/M output tokens; self-hosted sandboxes (public beta) enabling tool execution on user-controlled infrastructure (Cloudflare, Daytona, Modal, Vercel); MCP tunnels (research preview) for private-network MCP server access without public exposure; and a free real-time security-guidance plugin for Claude Code that detects ~25 high-risk vulnerability classes via regex pattern matching, shown to cut security-related PR comments by 30-40%.
updates.accuracy_notes: Official Anthropic source — considered authoritative for all feature claims, pricing, and benchmarks. Features marked 'research preview' and 'public beta' carry the usual early-access caveats. The 30-40% reduction in security PR comments is an internal Anthropic metric and cannot be independently verified yet.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.156, v2.1.157, v2.1.158 — Three Releases in 48 Hours
updates.summary: Three Claude Code releases shipped in rapid succession over May 29-30. v2.1.156 (May 29, 01:42 UTC) fixed a critical Opus 4.8 bug where modified thinking blocks caused API errors. v2.1.157 (May 29, 20:20 UTC) was the most substantial: it introduced plugin auto-loading from .claude/skills directories without requiring marketplace installation, added 'claude plugin init <name>' to scaffold new plugins, improved agent settings so the 'agent' field in settings.json is honored for dispatched sessions, enabled mid-session worktree switching via EnterWorktree, fixed unprocessable images crashing requests, resolved sandbox network permission prompts in auto/bypass mode, and fixed background agent worktree orphaning. v2.1.158 (May 30, 02:42 UTC) enabled Auto mode on Bedrock, Vertex, and Foundry for both Opus 4.7 and 4.8 (opt-in via CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1).
updates.accuracy_notes: Data pulled directly from official GitHub release notes (github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases) — considered authoritative. Version numbers, timestamps, and feature descriptions are verbatim from release notes. Auto mode on cloud providers is opt-in and may have additional prerequisites.

updates.title: Anthropic raises $65B in Series H funding at $965B post-money valuation
updates.summary: Anthropic announced a $65 billion Series H funding round led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, with co-leads including Capital Group, Coatue, D1 Capital Partners, GIC, ICONIQ, and XN. The round values Anthropic at $965 billion post-money — surpassing OpenAI and making it the most valuable private AI startup in the world, within striking distance of a $1 trillion valuation. The funding includes $15 billion from hyperscalers (including $5B from Amazon) and strategic infrastructure partners Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. Anthropic's run-rate revenue crossed $47 billion in May 2026 (up from Series G in February). Funds will advance safety and interpretability research, expand compute capacity for Claude, and scale products and partnerships. The Verge, TechCrunch, CNBC, Axios, and Fortune all covered this story on May 28; XDA did not publish a dedicated article.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed by official Anthropic press release plus corroborating coverage from TechCrunch, CNBC, Axios, Fortune, and GIC Newsroom. Valuation ($965B), funding amount ($65B), and lead investors are consistent across all sources. Revenue figure ($47B run-rate) is Anthropic's own disclosure. NOTE: This story is NOT from a monitored source (XDA, docs.anthropic.com, or The Verge) — included here because it is the single biggest Anthropic story of 2026 and would be a major content opportunity.
